checkpoint_context.active_numbering_note: The active checkpoint path reopens MULTI_VERSION_COEXISTENCE-0 as Φ-B3 after completed Υ-A even where older planning mirrors still drift.

coexistence_definition.summary: Multi-version coexistence is the declared, bounded, law-governed simultaneous presence, availability, interpretability, or resolvability of more than one version-distinguished runtime or release-control subject without allowing version multiplicity to redefine semantic truth, ownership, compatibility, lifecycle legality, or control-plane authority.
why_needed: The repo already contains replay, snapshot, lifecycle, release-resolution, manifest, trust, and operator-transaction substrate that can be misread as ad hoc coexistence permission.
Coexistence needs explicit law so side-by-side presence does not become semantic drift, authority drift, or control-plane folklore.
Later hotswap, distributed-authority, and Zeta continuity work need a bounded coexistence floor rather than implementation-specific hacks.

coexistence_subject_classes.description: Version-distinguished bounded component realizations that remain subordinate to component, state, lifecycle, and isolation law.
Version-distinguished runtime services whose mediation or routing role must not become semantic ownership.
Execution-hosting or continuity-support structures whose implementation version may differ while the semantic and lifecycle floors remain explicit.
Release identities, manifests, component graphs, install plans, indices, and archive records that may coexist as governed artifacts.
Semantic contract pins, release contract profiles, protocol ranges, schema ranges, format ranges, and target descriptors.
Selected, pinned, deprecated, yanked, superseded, or rollback-eligible control-plane states.
Schema or format families that may coexist only under explicit compatibility, refusal, or migration law.
Target-distinguished distribution or archive lanes whose exact target identity remains explicit.

allowed_coexistence_classes.description: Multiple release identities, manifests, archive entries, and superseded or yanked records may coexist as governed historical facts.
Multiple versioned component or release candidates may coexist in release-index and resolver surfaces.
Current, prior, rollback-target, pinned, deprecated, yanked, and superseded states may coexist as control-plane facts.
Multiple schema or format families may coexist under explicit schema, refusal, and migration law.
Version-distinguished runtime realizations may coexist only inside bounded identity, lifecycle, state, replay, snapshot, and isolation envelopes.
Different exact-target artifacts may coexist across release, archive, and mirror surfaces.

gated_or_prohibited_coexistence_classes.description: Two version-distinguished runtime subjects simultaneously mutating the same authoritative truth scope by convenience.
Different semantic meanings treated as interchangeable because product versions, files, or packaging look similar.
Coexistence used to smuggle restartless replacement, partial hot reload, or live cutover semantics.
Multiple versions sharing or racing authority envelopes without later distributed-authority law.
Treating field/, schemas/, generated feeds, or other projected surfaces as lawful peer coexistence lanes against canonical owners.
Treating archive presence, mirror presence, or generated feed presence as permission for live coexistence.
Version-distinguished runtime realizations coexist while relying on hidden service-local or component-local truth.
A coexistence claim whose legality depends on publication approval, trust-root posture, or licensing posture.

distinction_notes.coexistence: Bounded simultaneous presence, availability, interpretability, or resolvability under explicit law.
distinction_notes.migration: A state, schema, or representation change process that may occur without lawful side-by-side presence.
distinction_notes.rollback: A governed restoration or reselection transaction against prior recorded state.
distinction_notes.hotswap: A later cutover and replacement problem, not generic coexistence law.
distinction_notes.archive_retention: Historical preservation without automatic live admissibility.
distinction_notes.mirror_availability: Distribution or availability exposure without canonical authority.
distinction_notes.simple_storage_colocation: A file-layout fact that does not itself prove lawful coexistence.

invalidity_failure_categories.description: The coexistence scope is explicit and satisfies the declared constraints.
Coexistence may be discussable but requires explicit human review before stronger claims.
Evidence of coexistence exists, but compatibility or continuity proof is incomplete.
The explicit compatibility envelope is missing, incomplete, or contradictory.
The versions imply incompatible semantic meaning or erased bridge semantics.
The coexistence claim conflicts with ownership review or canonical-root discipline.
Authoritative or continuity-relevant state is trapped or intertwined across versions unsafely.
Lawful replay or lawful snapshot interpretation would be broken or ambiguous.
The coexistence posture conflicts with lifecycle legality for the claim being made.
Containment boundaries are missing or insufficient.
Release-control coexistence bypasses release contract profile, target specificity, or trust posture.
A coexistence claim is being used to smuggle restartless replacement or live cutover semantics.
A coexistence claim silently introduces split authority or handoff assumptions.
